content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

January 18, 2005

Quinn M. Barrow
City Attorney, South El Monte
Richards, Watson and Gershon
355 South Grand Avenue, 40th Floor
Los Angeles, CA 90071

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-04-246

Dear Mr. Barrow:

This letter confirms the receipt of your November 9, 2004, request for advice regarding a conflict of interest issue use of Councilmember Allen Co. Under the provisions of California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(8)(B), unless you are an authorized representative of the council member for which you seek assistance, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis. During a phone conversation with me, you indicated that you were not an authorized representative of this council member. I enclose a copy of regulation 18329 for your review.

If you have any questions, feel free to call me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

[illegible]

By: Trish Mayer
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

sections.conclusion: Under California Code of Regulations 18329(bX8)(B), the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address the questions or provide analysis unless the requestor is an authorized representative of the council member for which assistance is sought.
sections.question_synthetic: Can the FPPC provide advice regarding a conflict of interest issue involving Councilmember Allen Co. when the requestor is not an authorized representative of the council member?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice because the requestor is not an authorized representative of Councilmember Allen Co., as required by regulation 18329(bX8)(B).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter without traditional QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections. The legal question and FPPC conclusion were synthesized from the letter's body text. File number W-04-246 indicates this may have been a withdrawal or declination (W prefix).

QUESTION: Can the FPPC provide advice regarding a conflict of interest issue involving Councilmember Allen Co. when the requestor is not an authorized representative of the council member?

CONCLUSION: Under California Code of Regulations 18329(bX8)(B), the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address the questions or provide analysis unless the requestor is an authorized representative of the council member for which assistance is sought.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined a request for advice regarding a conflict of interest issue involving Councilmember Allen Co. because the requestor, the City Attorney, was not an authorized representative of the council member.
